questions.text: I can tell when a conversation is being held in a specific location because that location sends a message.
I feel things at a pitch that sometimes scares me.
I feel satisfaction in maintaining streaks and unbroken chains of consistent effort.
I feel that lending money I may never get back is preferable to watching someone struggle.
I find that I can discuss the most difficult aspects of the human experience without losing my analytical perspective.
I pick up skills quickly through trial and error rather than structured practice.
I pick up on the subtle ways people signal agreement or dissent without speaking.
I routinely set aside time and money specifically for my own growth and enjoyment.
I hold the view that the seriousness I bring to others' suffering is among the most important things I can cultivate in myself.
I believe that the pull toward acting on behalf of those who are suffering is the most trustworthy moral signal I can follow.
I experience anticipation as an almost unbearable physical buzz.
I notice that when I laugh, I laugh with my whole body — it takes me over completely.
I believe that patience and persistence are more important than talent in achieving mastery.
I would postpone my own goals indefinitely if someone close to me needed my help.
I finalize plans early to give myself and others time to prepare.
I routinely decline social events that don't advance my personal interests.
I believe that being too systematic can blind you to novel solutions hiding in plain sight.
I feel that building my personal brand is a smart investment of time.
I find that my emotions are like a steady current — always flowing but never flooding.
I focus on the content of what someone says rather than how they say it.
I enjoy the sense of being indispensable to a project's direction.
I notice that I frequently steer conversations toward inclusion.
I notice when a person places themselves in someone's line of sight to ensure they are seen.
I find that I make better decisions for others when I evaluate their circumstances without being influenced by how their situation makes me feel.
I believe that the obligation to respond to suffering should override competing pressures whenever the two come into conflict.
I find that I can engage with the most emotionally charged material and still feel centered when I walk away.
I feel that seeking input before acting is a sign of strength.
I change my work environment often because new surroundings stimulate fresh thinking.
I often initiate projects without waiting for approval.
I find that approaching others' pain with rational precision allows me to be more useful than approaching it with shared emotion.
I rarely feel emotionally overwhelmed, even during genuinely difficult times.
I am the kind of person who writes the first draft and circulates it for comment.
I would rather let the team take extra time than force a call that divides people.
I prefer relationships where people tell me directly when something is wrong.
I find that I miss when a person's farewell is warmer or cooler than their greeting and what that shift means.
I find that when I receive a compliment, my first thought is who else deserves one more.
